hero.title: Protect Your Crops with CropGuard AI
hero.subtitle: An intelligent, multilingual plant disease detection system designed for Ethiopian smallholder farmers. Upload a leaf image and receive instant AI-powered diagnosis with actionable treatment recommendations.
hero.supportedCrops: Supports Wheat · Teff · Maize · Tomato · Potato · Pepper

features.title: Why CropGuard AI?
features.subtitle: Purpose-built for Ethiopian smallholder farmers — accurate, accessible, and multilingual.

ai.title: AI Disease Detection
ai.desc: MobileNetV2-powered deep learning model trained on 14,559 leaf images achieves 93.7% classification accuracy across 10 crop disease classes.

multilingual.title: 3-Language Support
multilingual.desc: Full native support for English, Amharic (አማርኛ), and Tigrinya (ትግርኛ) — eliminating language barriers for Ethiopian farmers.

instant.title: Instant Results
instant.desc: Upload a leaf photo and receive a diagnosis in seconds, complete with confidence scores and tailored treatment recommendations.

history.title: Prediction History
history.desc: Track all your past scans in a personal dashboard. Monitor disease trends across your fields over time.

chat.title: AI Chat Assistant
chat.desc: Ask follow-up questions about any diagnosed disease in your preferred language. Powered by advanced natural language AI.

crops.title: Local Crop Coverage
crops.desc: Disease detection tailored for Tigray-prevalent crops: Wheat, Teff, Maize, Tomato, Potato, and Pepper.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three simple steps to protect your harvest

step1.title: Upload a Leaf Photo

step3.title: Get Recommendations

hero.title: About CropGuard AI
hero.subtitle: A research project from Mekelle University's Institute of Technology, developed to empower Ethiopian farmers with AI-driven agricultural diagnostics.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: Agriculture constitutes the cornerstone of Ethiopia's economy, employing approximately 70–80% of the national workforce. In the Tigray region, smallholder subsistence farming of staple crops such as wheat, teff, maize, sorghum, barley, and lentils represents the primary livelihood for millions of households. CropGuard AI was created to address the critical gap in accessible, accurate, and multilingual plant disease detection tools for these farming communities.
mission.quote: Empowering every farmer — regardless of language or location — with the diagnostic power of AI.
mission.card_subtitle: AI-powered crop disease detection for Ethiopian farmers

problem.title: The Problem We Solve
problem.subtitle: Four interconnected challenges face smallholder farmers in Tigray and across Ethiopia

items.title: Expert Unavailability
items.desc: Agricultural extension officers and plant pathologists serve large, geographically dispersed rural populations, resulting in delayed or inaccessible consultations and uncontrolled disease spread.

items.title: Language Barriers
items.desc: Existing AI-based plant disease tools are developed exclusively in English, rendering them inaccessible to the millions of Ethiopian farmers who communicate in Amharic or Tigrinya.

items.title: Pesticide Misuse
items.desc: Misdiagnosis leads to the indiscriminate or incorrect application of pesticides, inflating production costs, polluting water sources, and accelerating resistance development.

items.title: Manual Diagnostic Error
items.desc: Visual identification of plant disease symptoms by non-expert farmers is frequently inaccurate, particularly in early disease stages where symptoms of distinct pathogens overlap.

model.title: The Technology Behind CropGuard AI
model.text: CropGuard AI uses MobileNetV2 — a lightweight convolutional neural network optimized for mobile and web deployment, developed by Google. Pre-trained on ImageNet's 1.28 million images, then fine-tuned on a curated dataset of 14,559 plant disease images from the PlantVillage repository, supplemented with locally collected Tigray field images, the model achieves 93.7% validation accuracy across 10 disease classes. The architecture's inverted residual blocks and depthwise separable convolutions allow fast inference even on resource-constrained servers.

comparison.title: How We Compare
comparison.subtitle: CropGuard AI uniquely combines multilingual support, local crop coverage, and a prediction history dashboard

team.title: The Research Team
team.subtitle: Seven Bachelor of Information Technology graduates from Mekelle Institute of Technology who built CropGuard AI as their final thesis project.
team.institution: Mekelle University – MIT, Department of Information Technology, Mekelle, Tigray, Ethiopia

hero.title: Contact Us
hero.subtitle: Have questions about CropGuard AI? We'd love to hear from farmers, researchers, and partners.

form.name: Full Name
form.subject: Subject
form.success: Message sent successfully! We will get back to you soon.

info.title: Get in Touch
info.response_time_text: We typically respond to inquiries within 2–3 business days. For urgent research matters, please mention it in your subject line.

items.q: Is CropGuard AI free to use?
items.a: Yes, CropGuard AI is completely free for all farmers and researchers.

items.q: Which crops does the system support?
items.a: We support Wheat, Teff, Maize, Tomato, Potato, and Pepper — the most common crops in the Tigray region.

items.q: Does the app work on mobile phones?
items.a: Yes, CropGuard AI is fully responsive and works on any modern smartphone or computer with a browser.

items.q: How accurate is the AI diagnosis?
items.a: Our MobileNetV2 model achieves 93.7% validation accuracy across 10 disease classes.

treatment_labels.lbl_chemical_warning: ⚠ Wear protective gloves and mask when applying chemicals. Follow label instructions.
treatment_labels.organic_blight: Copper hydroxide 3g/L · Neem oil 5mL/L every 7 days
treatment_labels.organic_rust: Sulphur dust 3g/L · Potassium bicarbonate 5g/L · Neem oil 5mL/L
treatment_labels.organic_mildew: Potassium bicarbonate 5g/L · Baking soda 4g/L + neem oil 5mL/L
treatment_labels.organic_mold: Neem oil 5mL/L · Trichoderma biopesticide per label
treatment_labels.organic_virus: No organic cure. Remove plants. Control vectors with neem oil.
treatment_labels.chemical_blight: Mancozeb 2.5g/L OR Cymoxanil+Mancozeb 2g/L · Repeat every 5–7 days
treatment_labels.chemical_rust: Tebuconazole 1mL/L OR Propiconazole 1mL/L · 2–3 apps, 14 days apart
treatment_labels.chemical_mildew: Trifloxystrobin 0.5g/L OR Myclobutanil 1g/L · At first sign

resultcard.disclaimer: This AI analysis is for guidance only. Consult a local agricultural expert for definitive diagnosis and treatment decisions.
resultcard.step1: Isolate affected plants immediately to prevent spread
resultcard.step2: Remove and safely dispose of heavily infected material
resultcard.step4: Monitor surrounding plants for signs of infection
resultcard.step5: Follow up with preventive measures after recovery

hero.title: Disease Guide
hero.subtitle: Comprehensive guide to crop diseases affecting Ethiopian agriculture — symptoms, treatments, and prevention

list.name: Wheat Leaf Rust
list.symptoms: Orange-brown powdery pustules on upper leaf surface. Leaves turn yellow and dry.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Tebuconazole or Propiconazole fungicide. Remove and destroy infected plants. Use resistant varieties.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid dense planting. Monitor during warm humid weather.

list.name: Wheat Stem Rust
list.symptoms: Dark red-brown pustules on stems and leaves. Severely weakens stems causing lodging.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Spray Propiconazole fungicide at first sign. Use certified resistant seed.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Avoid late planting. Remove volunteer wheat plants.

list.name: Wheat Yellow Rust
list.symptoms: Yellow-orange pustules in stripes along leaves. Leaves turn pale yellow.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Trifloxystrobin or Propiconazole early. Avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Monitor fields regularly in cool wet weather.

list.name: Northern Leaf Blight
list.symptoms: Long cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions (5–15 cm) on leaves with wavy edges.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Mancozeb or Azoxystrobin fungicide. Remove infected crop residue after harvest.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Practice crop rotation. Use resistant hybrids. Bury infected residue.

list.name: Gray Leaf Spot
list.symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions with yellow borders running parallel to leaf veins.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Use resistant hybrids. Apply Azoxystrobin or Propiconazole at early signs.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Rotate crops. Ensure good plant spacing. Bury crop residue after harvest.

list.name: Common Rust
list.symptoms: Small powdery brick-red pustules on both sides of leaves. Turn dark brown with age.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil if severe. Plant resistant hybrids.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Monitor during cool wet periods. Plant early-maturing resistant varieties.

list.name: Sorghum Anthracnose
list.symptoms: Red or tan oval spots on leaves and stalks. Spots have dark red borders. Stalk rot in severe cases.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Use certified disease-free seed. Apply Chlorothalonil. Destroy infected residue.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Rotate crops. Avoid dense planting.

list.name: Sorghum Downy Mildew
list.symptoms: White downy growth on underside of leaves. Leaves turn yellow and plant is stunted.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Treat seed with Metalaxyl before planting. Remove and destroy all infected plants.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Treat seed. Avoid fields with previous downy mildew.

list.name: Teff Leaf Spot
list.symptoms: Small brown circular spots on leaves. Spots may have yellow halos. Mild defoliation.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Improve plant spacing for airflow. Apply copper-based fungicide if severe.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation. Use well-drained soil. Rotate crops.

list.name: Tomato Early Blight
list.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric target-like rings on lower leaves. Yellow halo around spots.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Remove lower infected leaves. Stake plants for air circulation.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Rotate crops. Mulch around base to prevent soil splash. Avoid overhead watering.

list.name: Tomato Late Blight
list.symptoms: Water-soaked gray-green lesions that turn brown fast. White mold on leaf undersides. Fruit rots.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Mancozeb or Cymoxanil IMMEDIATELY — spreads extremely fast. Remove all infected parts.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Avoid wetting foliage. Use resistant varieties. Ensure good ventilation.

list.name: Tomato Bacterial Spot
list.symptoms: Small dark water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit. Spots become brown with yellow halos.
list.causes: Caused by bacterial pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply copper-based bactericide. Remove infected stems and leaves. Avoid overhead irrigation.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Use disease-free transplants. Avoid working with plants when wet. Rotate crops.

list.name: Tomato Leaf Mold
list.symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaf surface. Olive-green to brown fuzzy mold on leaf underside.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Improve ventilation. Apply Chlorothalonil or copper fungicide. Avoid wetting leaves.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Space plants well. Keep humidity below 85%. Use resistant varieties.

list.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
list.symptoms: Many small circular spots with dark borders and light gray centers. Tiny black dots inside spots.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Remove and destroy infected leaves.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Mulch soil surface. Avoid overhead irrigation. Rotate crops annually.

list.name: Spider Mites
list.symptoms: Tiny yellow or white speckles on leaves. Fine webbing on leaf undersides. Leaves dry and curl.
list.causes: Caused by pest pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Abamectin or Bifenazate miticide. Use neem oil as organic option. Increase humidity.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Keep plants well-watered. Introduce predatory mites. Avoid dusty conditions.

list.name: Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
list.symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of leaves. Stunted growth. Flowers drop without fruiting.
list.causes: Caused by viral pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: No cure. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control whiteflies with insecticides or sticky traps.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Use TYLCV-resistant varieties. Install insect-proof mesh. Monitor for whiteflies.

list.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
list.symptoms: Mottled light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves. Distorted fern-like new growth.
list.causes: Caused by viral pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: No cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Wash hands before handling. Use certified disease-free transplants.

list.name: Tomato Target Spot
list.symptoms: Brown circular spots with concentric rings on leaves and fruit. Premature leaf drop.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Chlorothalonil or Azoxystrobin. Remove heavily infected leaves.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Improve air circulation through staking. Avoid overhead irrigation.

list.name: Potato Early Blight
list.symptoms: Brown spots with yellow halo and target ring pattern on older lower leaves.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Spray Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Ensure good drainage and adequate potassium.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Rotate crops. Mulch to reduce soil splash. Avoid drought stress.

list.name: Potato Late Blight
list.symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions on leaves. White mold on underside. Tubers develop brown rot.
list.causes: Caused by fungal pathogens. Spreads in warm, humid conditions.
list.chemical: Apply Mancozeb or Cymoxanil immediately. Remove all infected plant material.
list.organic: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based organic sprays. Ensure proper crop spacing and airflow.
list.prevention: Use certified seed potatoes. Avoid overhead irrigation. Scout fields regularly.

aiChat.language_hint_en: Type in English, Amharic (አማርኛ), or Tigrinya (ትግርኛ)

aiChat.footer_disclaimer_am: Enter ለመላክ · AI ሁልጊዜ ትክክል አይሆንም — ጥርጣሬ ካለ ባለሙያ ያማክሩ
aiChat.footer_disclaimer_ti: Enter ን ሰደድ · AI ኩሉ ጊዜ ቅኑዕ ኣይኮነን — ምስ ሙኩር ክትዘራረብ
aiChat.greeting_en: Hello! I'm Sage, your CropGuard AI agricultural assistant. I can help with crop diseases, pests, soil health, fertilizers, irrigation, and farming best practices. What can I help you with today?
aiChat.greeting_am: ሰላም! እኔ ሴጅ ነኝ፣ የ CropGuard AI የእርሻ አማካሪ። በሰብል በሽታዎች፣ ተባዮች፣ ማዳበሪያ እና ስለ እርሻ ጥያቄ ቢኖርዎ እዚህ ነኝ። ምን ልርዳዎ?
aiChat.greeting_ti: ሰላም! ኣነ ሴጅ እየ፣ ናይ CropGuard AI ናይ ሕርሻ ምኽሪ ሃቢ። ብዛዕባ ሕማም ሕርሻ፣ ኣባ ጎሮ፣ ፍሩቅ ወይ ካሊእ ሕቶ ኣለካ? ሓግዘካ ኢየ።

resultCard.disclaimer: AI-assisted preliminary diagnosis only. For severe outbreaks, consult your agricultural extension officer.

feedbackPanel.step1_subtitle: Your feedback helps improve the AI model for all farmers.
feedbackPanel.placeholder_wrong: e.g. It was actually Tomato Early Blight, not Late Blight...
feedbackPanel.placeholder_correct: Any notes about symptoms, field conditions, or treatment used...
feedbackPanel.step3_subtitle: Your feedback has been submitted and helps improve AI accuracy for all farmers.
feedbackPanel.toast_submitted: Thank you! Your feedback improves our AI.
feedbackPanel.feedback_thanks_sub: Your response helps improve AI accuracy for all farmers.
feedbackPanel.feedback_note_ph_wrong: e.g. It was actually Tomato Early Blight, not Late Blight…
feedbackPanel.feedback_note_ph_right: Any notes about symptoms, conditions, or treatment used…

smartRecommendation.no_action_desc: This crop appears healthy. Continue regular monitoring every 3–5 days during high-risk periods.
smartRecommendation.tip_add_farm: 💡 Tip: Add this crop to your Farm Manager with its growth stage for more personalised treatment recommendations.

medium.escalation: organic first, then chemical if no improvement in 5 days
medium.label: 🟡 Moderate

stage_strategies.Seedling: Seedlings are fragile — avoid strong chemicals. Organic options are safer at this stage.
stage_strategies.Vegetative: Early vegetative stage — start organic. Escalate to chemical only if disease spreads beyond 25% of canopy.
stage_strategies.Flowering: Flowering stage — minimise chemical applications to protect pollinators. Target treatment to evenings.
stage_strategies.Fruiting: Fruiting stage — chemical may be necessary but observe pre-harvest intervals carefully.
stage_strategies.Harvest: Near harvest — avoid all chemical applications. Harvest healthy portions first.
stage_strategies.Dormant: Dormant season — ideal time for preventive chemical treatment and pruning.

crop_notes.Tomato: Tomatoes are susceptible to a wide range of diseases. Monitor twice weekly during humid periods.
crop_notes.Corn: Corn diseases spread rapidly. Scout the entire field when symptoms are first detected.
crop_notes.Apple: Apple diseases require preventive spray programmes starting from bud break.
crop_notes.Grape: Grapes are especially vulnerable to fungal diseases. Canopy management is key.
crop_notes.Potato: Late Blight in potato can destroy an entire crop in days. Act immediately.
crop_notes.Pepper: Bacterial diseases in pepper spread via water splash — avoid overhead irrigation.
crop_notes.Peach: Peach bacterial spot — use resistant varieties for future planting.
crop_notes.Strawberry: Strawberry diseases are often linked to poor drainage and air circulation.
crop_notes.Squash: Powdery mildew on squash responds well to organic treatments applied early.
crop_notes.Orange: Citrus greening has no cure — focus on prevention and vector control.
crop_notes.default: Follow integrated pest management practices for best long-term results.

medium_low.label: Start organic
medium_low.desc: Apply neem oil or copper-based organic fungicide. Monitor for 5 days.

medium_low.label: Escalate if needed
medium_low.desc: If no improvement after 5 days, switch to {escalation}.

medium_low.label: Consult expert
medium_low.desc: If symptoms worsen despite treatment, contact your agricultural extension officer.

high.label: Immediate chemical treatment
high.desc: Severity is high — organic options may be too slow. Apply chemical fungicide within 24h.

high.label: Isolate and remove
high.desc: Remove and bag all severely infected plant material immediately.

high.label: Notify extension office
high.desc: High-severity outbreaks should be reported for regional tracking.

explainPanel.section_ai_detected: 🔬 What the AI detected in this leaf
explainPanel.section_how_ai_works: ⚙ How the AI reached this result
explainPanel.limitations_notice: ⚠ Limitations: AI accuracy depends on photo quality, lighting and crop growth stage. Early-stage diseases may produce lower confidence. Always confirm severe diagnoses with an agricultural extension officer.
explainPanel.margin_weak: — consider taking a clearer photo to increase certainty.
explainPanel.model_trained_note: 🏋 Model trained on 54,305 leaf images across 14 crops and 38 disease classes using MobileNetV2 transfer learning. Accuracy target: ≥85%.

high.label: Very High Confidence
high.text: The AI found strong visual evidence. Multiple disease markers clearly visible and consistent with known patterns.

good.label: High Confidence
good.text: Clear visual evidence detected. Dominant features match this disease well. Minor ambiguity in secondary markers.

medium.label: Moderate Confidence
medium.text: Partial evidence detected. Some markers are present but image quality or early-stage symptoms create some uncertainty.

low.label: Low Confidence
low.text: Limited evidence. The model detected possible signs but strongly recommends a clearer photograph or expert consultation.

model_steps.label: Leaf segmentation
model_steps.desc: Model isolated the leaf from background

model_steps.label: Texture analysis
model_steps.desc: Analysed lesion shape, size and distribution

riskScorePanel.early_treatment_note: 💡 With early treatment: Yield loss can be reduced to approximately {min}–{max}% through prompt intervention.
riskScorePanel.repeat_detection: 🔁 Repeat detection: This disease has appeared in {n} recent scan(s). Indicates active disease pressure in your field — widen your scouting area.

yield_impact_notes.blight: Blights can cause catastrophic yield loss if untreated.
yield_impact_notes.rust: Rust diseases reduce grain/fruit quality and quantity.
yield_impact_notes.mildew: Powdery mildew affects photosynthesis and fruit quality.
yield_impact_notes.mold: Leaf mold significantly reduces fruit set and quality.
yield_impact_notes.virus: Viral diseases have no cure and can devastate entire crops.
yield_impact_notes.default: Disease impact varies — early treatment minimises losses.

locationIntelligence.error_denied: Location access denied. Using Mekelle default.

risk_rules.message: Very high fungal risk — humidity above 85% with warm temperatures create ideal conditions for blight and mould.
Moderate fungal risk — current humidity favours powdery mildew and leaf mould development.
Elevated spore dispersal risk — high wind speeds can carry rust and blight spores across fields.
Hot dry conditions favour spider mite outbreaks. Check leaf undersides carefully.
Low humidity reduces fungal risk. Monitor for pest activity instead.
Current conditions present low disease risk. Continue regular monitoring.

correlation_tips.humidity_high: Humidity {value}% → Fungal disease risk is elevated
correlation_tips.humidity_med: Humidity {value}% → Moderate fungal risk, monitor closely
correlation_tips.heat_dry: Hot & dry conditions → Spider mite and pest pressure elevated
correlation_tips.recent_rain: Recent rainfall → Increases late blight and bacterial disease risk
correlation_tips.high_wind: High winds → Spores dispersing, widen your scouting area

diseaseEncyclopedia.no_diseases_found: No diseases found. Try a different search or filter.
diseaseEncyclopedia.enc_sub: Reference guide for crop diseases — symptoms, causes and treatments

diseases.name: Wheat Leaf Rust
diseases.symptom: Orange-brown powdery pustules on upper leaf surface. Leaves turn yellow and dry.
diseases.treatment: Apply Tebuconazole or Propiconazole fungicide. Remove and destroy infected plants. Use resistant varieties.
diseases.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid dense planting. Monitor during warm humid weather.

diseases.name: Wheat Stem Rust
diseases.symptom: Dark red-brown pustules on stems and leaves. Severely weakens stems causing lodging.
diseases.treatment: Spray Propiconazole fungicide at first sign. Use certified resistant seed.
diseases.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Avoid late planting. Remove volunteer wheat plants.

diseases.name: Wheat Yellow Rust
diseases.symptom: Yellow-orange pustules in stripes along leaves. Leaves turn pale yellow.
diseases.treatment: Apply Trifloxystrobin or Propiconazole early. Avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer.
diseases.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Monitor fields regularly in cool wet weather.

diseases.name: Northern Leaf Blight
diseases.symptom: Long cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions (5–15 cm) on leaves with wavy edges.
diseases.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Azoxystrobin fungicide. Remove infected crop residue after harvest.
diseases.prevention: Practice crop rotation. Use resistant hybrids. Bury infected residue.

diseases.name: Gray Leaf Spot
diseases.symptom: Rectangular gray to tan lesions with yellow borders running parallel to leaf veins.
diseases.treatment: Use resistant hybrids. Apply Azoxystrobin or Propiconazole at early signs.
diseases.prevention: Rotate crops. Ensure good plant spacing. Bury crop residue after harvest.

diseases.name: Common Rust
diseases.symptom: Small powdery brick-red pustules on both sides of leaves. Turn dark brown with age.
diseases.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil if severe. Plant resistant hybrids.
diseases.prevention: Monitor during cool wet periods. Plant early-maturing resistant varieties.

diseases.name: Sorghum Anthracnose
diseases.symptom: Red or tan oval spots on leaves and stalks. Spots have dark red borders. Stalk rot in severe cases.
diseases.treatment: Use certified disease-free seed. Apply Chlorothalonil. Destroy infected residue.
diseases.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Rotate crops. Avoid dense planting.

diseases.name: Sorghum Downy Mildew
diseases.symptom: White downy growth on underside of leaves. Leaves turn yellow and plant is stunted.
diseases.treatment: Treat seed with Metalaxyl before planting. Remove and destroy all infected plants.
diseases.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Treat seed. Avoid fields with previous downy mildew.

diseases.name: Teff Leaf Spot
diseases.symptom: Small brown circular spots on leaves. Spots may have yellow halos. Mild defoliation.
diseases.treatment: Improve plant spacing for airflow. Apply copper-based fungicide if severe.
diseases.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation. Use well-drained soil. Rotate crops.

diseases.name: Tomato Early Blight
diseases.symptom: Dark brown spots with concentric target-like rings on lower leaves. Yellow halo around spots.
diseases.treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Remove lower infected leaves. Stake plants for air circulation.
diseases.prevention: Rotate crops. Mulch around base to prevent soil splash. Avoid overhead watering.

diseases.name: Tomato Late Blight
diseases.symptom: Water-soaked gray-green lesions that turn brown fast. White mold on leaf undersides. Fruit rots.
diseases.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Cymoxanil IMMEDIATELY — spreads extremely fast. Remove all infected parts.
diseases.prevention: Avoid wetting foliage. Use resistant varieties. Ensure good ventilation.

diseases.name: Tomato Bacterial Spot
diseases.symptom: Small dark water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit. Spots become brown with yellow halos.
diseases.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide. Remove infected stems and leaves. Avoid overhead irrigation.
diseases.prevention: Use disease-free transplants. Avoid working with plants when wet. Rotate crops.

diseases.name: Tomato Leaf Mold
diseases.symptom: Yellow patches on upper leaf surface. Olive-green to brown fuzzy mold on leaf underside.
diseases.treatment: Improve ventilation. Apply Chlorothalonil or copper fungicide. Avoid wetting leaves.
diseases.prevention: Space plants well. Keep humidity below 85%. Use resistant varieties.

diseases.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
diseases.symptom: Many small circular spots with dark borders and light gray centers. Tiny black dots inside spots.
diseases.treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Remove and destroy infected leaves.
diseases.prevention: Mulch soil surface. Avoid overhead irrigation. Rotate crops annually.

diseases.name: Spider Mites
diseases.symptom: Tiny yellow or white speckles on leaves. Fine webbing on leaf undersides. Leaves dry and curl.
diseases.treatment: Apply Abamectin or Bifenazate miticide. Use neem oil as organic option. Increase humidity.
diseases.prevention: Keep plants well-watered. Introduce predatory mites. Avoid dusty conditions.

diseases.name: Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
diseases.symptom: Upward curling and yellowing of leaves. Stunted growth. Flowers drop without fruiting.
diseases.treatment: No cure. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control whiteflies with insecticides or sticky traps.
diseases.prevention: Use TYLCV-resistant varieties. Install insect-proof mesh. Monitor for whiteflies.

diseases.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
diseases.symptom: Mottled light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves. Distorted fern-like new growth.
diseases.treatment: No cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution.
diseases.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Wash hands before handling. Use certified disease-free transplants.

diseases.name: Tomato Target Spot
diseases.symptom: Brown circular spots with concentric rings on leaves and fruit. Premature leaf drop.
diseases.treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Azoxystrobin. Remove heavily infected leaves.
diseases.prevention: Improve air circulation through staking. Avoid overhead irrigation.

diseases.name: Potato Early Blight
diseases.symptom: Brown spots with yellow halo and target ring pattern on older lower leaves.
diseases.treatment: Spray Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Ensure good drainage and adequate potassium.
diseases.prevention: Rotate crops. Mulch to reduce soil splash. Avoid drought stress.

diseases.name: Potato Late Blight
diseases.symptom: Dark water-soaked lesions on leaves. White mold on underside. Tubers develop brown rot.
diseases.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Cymoxanil immediately. Remove all infected plant material.
diseases.prevention: Use certified seed potatoes. Avoid overhead irrigation. Scout fields regularly.

communityPage.outbreak_desc: {n} farmers in Tigray have reported this disease in the last week. Monitor your crops closely.
communityPage.watching_symptoms: ⏳ Treatment not yet applied — watching symptoms

communityPage.experts_intro: Verified tips from plant pathologists and agricultural extension officers in Ethiopia.
communityPage.community_sub: See what diseases other farmers in Tigray are reporting.

expert_tips_data.tip: Late blight spreads within 48 hours in humid conditions. Never wait — apply Mancozeb the same day you spot symptoms.
For rust diseases in wheat and corn: scout early mornings when dew is present — spores are most visible then.
Neem oil loses effectiveness after 8 hours in sunlight. Always apply in the evening for maximum coverage.

diseaseMeta.Apple___Apple_scab: Apply sulfur spray or neem oil at first sign. Remove affected leaves.
diseaseMeta.Apple___Black_rot: Prune infected wood. Apply copper-based fungicide. Remove mummified fruit.
diseaseMeta.Apple___Cedar_apple_rust: Remove nearby cedar/juniper hosts. Apply sulfur fungicide.
diseaseMeta.Apple___healthy: No treatment needed. Maintain orchard hygiene.
diseaseMeta.Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew: Apply potassium bicarbonate or neem oil. Improve air circulation.
diseaseMeta.Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray: Rotate crops. Remove crop debris. Apply neem oil at early stages.
diseaseMeta.Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_: Plant resistant varieties. Neem oil at early stage.
diseaseMeta.Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight: Rotate crops. Remove infected debris. Copper spray at early signs.
diseaseMeta.Grape___Black_rot: Remove mummified berries. Apply copper fungicide. Improve canopy airflow.
diseaseMeta.Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles): No cure. Remove infected vines. Seal pruning wounds with paste.
diseaseMeta.Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo: Apply copper spray. Remove infected leaves. Improve airflow.
diseaseMeta.Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening): No cure. Remove infected tree immediately. Control psyllid insects.
diseaseMeta.Peach___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper bactericide. Avoid overhead irrigation.
diseaseMeta.Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper spray. Remove infected leaves. Use disease-free transplants.
diseaseMeta.Potato___Early_blight: Remove lower infected leaves. Apply copper-based fungicide. Rotate crops.
diseaseMeta.Potato___Late_blight: ⚠️ Act immediately. Remove all infected material. Copper spray. Destroy infected plants.
diseaseMeta.Potato___healthy: No treatment needed. Scout regularly during wet weather.
diseaseMeta.Squash___Powdery_mildew: Apply baking soda spray (1 tsp/L water) or neem oil. Improve airflow.
diseaseMeta.Strawberry___Leaf_scorch: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper fungicide. Avoid wetting foliage.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper bactericide. Remove infected leaves. Avoid wet foliage.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Early_blight: Remove lower infected leaves. Apply copper fungicide or neem oil. Mulch base.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Late_blight: ⚠️ Act immediately. Remove infected parts. Apply copper spray. Destroy severely infected plants.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Leaf_Mold: Improve ventilation. Reduce humidity. Apply copper or sulfur spray.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper spray. Mulch soil to reduce splash.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider: Spray with neem oil or insecticidal soap. Increase humidity. Introduce predatory mites.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Target_Spot: Remove heavily infected leaves. Apply neem oil. Stake plants for airflow.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus: No cure. Remove infected plants immediately. Use reflective mulch to deter whiteflies.
diseaseMeta.Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus: No cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools with bleach. Wash hands before handling.

recommendations.Apple___Apple_scab: Apply fungicide (Captan or Mancozeb) at green tip stage. Remove and destroy fallen infected leaves. Prune for good air circulation.
recommendations.Apple___Black_rot: Prune out infected cankers and mummified fruit. Apply Captan or Thiophanate-methyl fungicide. Remove all infected material from the orchard.
recommendations.Apple___Cedar_apple_rust: Apply myclobutanil or propiconazole fungicide starting at pink bud stage. Remove nearby cedar/juniper trees if possible.
recommendations.Apple___healthy: No disease detected. Maintain regular pruning, balanced fertilisation, and monitor for early signs of pests or disease.
recommendations.Blueberry___healthy: No disease detected. Maintain proper soil pH (4.5–5.5), adequate irrigation, and annual pruning.
recommendations.Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew: Apply sulphur-based or potassium bicarbonate fungicide. Improve air circulation by pruning. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilisation.
recommendations.Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy: No disease detected. Continue regular monitoring and maintain orchard hygiene.
recommendations.Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray: Apply Azoxystrobin or Propiconazole at early signs. Use resistant hybrids in future seasons. Rotate crops.
recommendations.Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil if infection is severe. Plant resistant hybrids. Monitor fields during cool wet periods.
recommendations.Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight: Apply Mancozeb or Azoxystrobin fungicide. Practice crop rotation. Bury or remove infected crop residue after harvest.
recommendations.Corn_(maize)___healthy: No disease detected. Continue monitoring and maintain good agronomic practices.
recommendations.Grape___Black_rot: Apply Mancozeb or Myclobutanil from bud break through harvest. Remove mummified berries and infected canes.
recommendations.Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles): No effective cure. Remove and destroy severely infected vines. Protect pruning wounds with fungicide paste.
recommendations.Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo: Apply copper-based fungicide or Mancozeb. Improve air circulation. Remove infected leaves promptly.
recommendations.Grape___healthy: No disease detected. Maintain canopy management and balanced nutrition.
recommendations.Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening): No cure. Remove and destroy infected trees immediately. Control the Asian citrus psyllid with insecticides. Plant certified disease-free trees.
recommendations.Peach___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper-based bactericide in autumn and early spring. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove infected twigs.
recommendations.Peach___healthy: No disease detected. Continue regular monitoring, proper pruning, and balanced fertilisation.
recommendations.Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper-based bactericide. Remove infected material. Avoid overhead irrigation. Use disease-free transplants.
recommendations.Pepper,_bell___healthy: No disease detected. Maintain proper spacing for air circulation.
recommendations.Potato___Early_blight: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Remove infected lower leaves. Ensure adequate potassium. Practice crop rotation.
recommendations.Potato___Late_blight: ⚠️ Apply Mancozeb or Cymoxanil IMMEDIATELY — late blight spreads very rapidly. Remove and destroy all infected plant material.
recommendations.Potato___healthy: No disease detected. Scout fields regularly, especially during cool wet weather.
recommendations.Raspberry___healthy: No disease detected. Maintain proper cane management and remove old canes after fruiting.
recommendations.Soybean___healthy: No disease detected. Monitor for soybean rust during the growing season.
recommendations.Squash___Powdery_mildew: Apply potassium bicarbonate, neem oil, or sulphur at first sign. Improve air circulation. Avoid wetting foliage.
recommendations.Strawberry___Leaf_scorch: Apply Captan or Myclobutanil. Remove infected leaves. Ensure good drainage and air circulation.
recommendations.Strawberry___healthy: No disease detected. Maintain proper bed renovation after harvest.
recommendations.Tomato___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper-based bactericide. Remove infected leaves and stems. Avoid overhead irrigation.
recommendations.Tomato___Early_blight: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Remove lower infected leaves. Stake plants for better air circulation.
recommendations.Tomato___Late_blight: ⚠️ Apply Mancozeb or Cymoxanil IMMEDIATELY — this disease spreads extremely fast. Remove infected parts.
recommendations.Tomato___Leaf_Mold: Improve ventilation. Apply Chlorothalonil or copper fungicide. Avoid wetting leaves.
recommendations.Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Remove infected lower leaves. Mulch around base to prevent soil splash.
recommendations.Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider: Apply Abamectin or Bifenazate miticide. Use neem oil as an organic option. Increase humidity.
recommendations.Tomato___Target_Spot: Apply Chlorothalonil or Azoxystrobin. Remove heavily infected leaves. Improve air circulation.
recommendations.Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus: No cure. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control the whitefly vector with sticky traps.
recommendations.Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus: No cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools with bleach solution. Wash hands before handling plants.
recommendations.Tomato___healthy: No disease detected. Continue regular scouting and maintain good cultural practices.

errors.image_quality_low: Image quality too low for reliable diagnosis.
errors.image_too_small: Image too small ({w}×{h}px). Please use a photo of at least {min}×{min} pixels.
errors.image_blurry: Image appears blurry. Please hold the camera steady and take a sharper photo.
errors.confidence_too_low: Confidence too low ({confidence}%). The model is unsure — the leaf may not be clearly visible, or the disease may not be in the supported list.
errors.retake_reason: Please retake the photo: ensure the leaf fills the frame, use good lighting, and focus on the most affected area.
errors.file_not_image: File must be an image (JPEG, PNG, or WebP).
errors.fallback_recommendation: Consult your local agricultural extension officer.
